Identity.Name: StingTheatreTypes
Identity.Definition: Operating-theatre type classification per HBN 26. Drives theatre-air-supply strategy (laminar vs turbulent), pressure regime selection, MGS terminal-unit complement, room data sheet template, and the OR-RCP drawing-type pack.

Governance.StandardsBasis: HBN 26 (Facilities for surgical procedures) 2013; HTM 03-01 ventilation; HFN 26-1 minor surgical procedure rooms

Value.Definition: Standard Theatre — general / day surgery. Turbulent-flow ventilation, +25 Pa positive, full MGS complement (O2, N2O, AIR-4, AIR-7, VAC, AGSS).
Ultra-Clean Theatre — orthopaedic / implant surgery. Laminar-flow canopy directly above operating table; high-efficiency HEPA filtration; +45 Pa positive cascade.
Hybrid Theatre — surgery + intraoperative imaging (CT, MRI, C-arm fluoroscopy). Combines theatre + imaging requirements; lead shielding in walls; MRI-zone considerations if MR-hybrid.
Bone Marrow Protection Theatre — extreme positive pressure (+45 Pa or higher). Highest air-change rate; HEPA + UV. For immunocompromised transplant patients.
Orthopaedic Theatre — general orthopaedic (non-laminar). Standard or upgraded HEPA. May or may not have ultra-clean canopy.
Hybrid Interventional Radiology Theatre — IR procedures with surgical contingency. C-arm + lead shielding + theatre-grade vent.
Cardiac Catheterisation Lab — coronary angiography + interventional cardiology. Theatre-grade air; full lead shielding; ECG / haemodynamic monitoring.
Caesarean Theatre — dedicated obstetric theatre. Often adjoining LDR rooms; warmer setpoint; entonox provision.
Labour-Delivery-Recovery Room — single-room maternity model; not strictly a theatre but managed under HBN 09 with theatre-adjacent provisions.
Minor Operations / Procedure Room (HFN 26-1). Local-anaesthetic procedures; reduced ventilation requirements; smaller MGS complement.
Maternity-Adjacent Theatre — emergency caesarean within delivery suite. Theatre-grade ventilation with rapid mobilisation.
